evals.name: rwo-multi-replica-pod-affinity
evals.prompt: I have a Deployment with 5 replicas that all need to read and write to the same 10Gi volume. My cluster uses Hetzner Cloud Volumes as the StorageClass. How do I set this up correctly?
evals.expected_output: Create a single PVC with accessModes: ReadWriteOnce against the hcloud-volumes StorageClass, then add a podAffinity rule using preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution with topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname to co-locate all Pods on the same node. Do not use requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution because a hard self-referential affinity deadlocks the first deploy — no node has an app Pod yet, so all Pods stay Pending. If cross-node spread is needed, switch to Longhorn which supports RWX.
expectations: Uses preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution (soft) podAffinity, not requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution (hard), and explains the first-deploy deadlock risk of the hard variant
Specifies topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname in the podAffinityTerm to co-locate Pods on the same node
Sets accessModes: ReadWriteOnce on the PVC because Hetzner Cloud Volumes do not support RWX
Mentions Longhorn as the alternative that enables RWX and allows Pods to spread across nodes without podAffinity
Avoids recommending ReadWriteMany on Hetzner Cloud Volumes, which does not support that access mode

evals.name: statefulset-per-replica-volumes
evals.prompt: I'm deploying a PostgreSQL StatefulSet with 3 replicas. Each replica needs its own 20Gi volume. Should I use a single PVC in the Deployment spec or volumeClaimTemplates in a StatefulSet? Show me the right approach.
evals.expected_output: Use StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates — each Pod gets a dedicated PVC named data-cache-0, data-cache-1, data-cache-2, so replicas can schedule independently on different nodes with no podAffinity required. Warn that kubectl delete statefulset does not delete the PVCs; they must be deleted explicitly by name after verifying with kubectl get pvc. Avoid kubectl delete pvc -l app=cache because label-based bulk delete can affect other PVCs in the namespace.
expectations: Recommends StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates, not Deployment + single shared PVC, because each DB replica needs independent state
States that volumeClaimTemplates auto-names PVCs as <template-name>-<statefulset-name>-<ordinal> (e.g., data-cache-0)
Explicitly warns that deleting the StatefulSet does NOT delete the PVCs and they must be deleted by name
Warns against kubectl delete pvc -l <label> because label-based bulk delete can hit unintended PVCs in the namespace
Notes that no podAffinity is needed because each Pod has its own PVC and can schedule on any node

evals.name: secret-volume-mount-rotation
evals.prompt: My app reads a database password from a ConfigMap env var today. My security team says this is wrong. What's the correct way to handle this, and how do I make sure Pods pick up the new value when the secret changes?
evals.expected_output: Move the password to a Secret (type Opaque) and mount it as a volume with readOnly: true rather than using envFrom, because env vars are visible in crash dumps, /proc, and docker inspect. Volume-mounted Secrets update on disk within ~1 minute (kubelet sync), but most apps don't re-read files at runtime, so a rollout restart is still needed. For automated restarts on Secret changes, use the Reloader controller. For Helm-managed workloads, add a checksum/config annotation to the pod template.
expectations: Moves the credential from ConfigMap to Secret, citing that Secrets can be encrypted at rest while ConfigMaps cannot
Recommends mounting the Secret as a volume with readOnly: true rather than envFrom, because env vars leak into crash dumps and /proc
Explains that envFrom env vars never update at runtime — they are set at container start — so a rollout restart is always required
Names at least one specific update strategy from the skill: kubectl rollout restart, Reloader controller, hash-based naming with --append-hash, or checksum/config Helm annotation
Avoids recommending ConfigMap for the database password
